### Jack Kruse — 2026-05-07T22:07:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k-jabs-in-hospital-at-birth-hard-pass.32718/post-373363>

Here is the first-principles breakdown of why the Vitamin K bolus (the shot) contradicts the "hardware requirements" of a newborn:

**1\. The Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE) & Glutathione**
The slides highlights that the placenta and mammary glands **concentrate and slowly release MK-4**.

**The Detox Trap:** Converting Vitamin K1 or synthetic K variants into MK-4 requires the intermediate **menadione**. This process is a "metabolic tax" that consumes **glutathione**. Not good.

**The Isotopic Stress:** Glutathione is the primary defense against **Deuterium** and oxidative stress. By forcing a massive bolus of K1 into a neonate, medicine triggers a "glutathione drain." This is what acetomenphen does to adults. This is especially concerning when a kid gets a jab fever and the pediatrician tells mom to give the kid tylenol. It is psychotic the adivce that become evidence based when you understand Physics > Pharma is the lesson of the Wisest. In a high-D environment (like a modern nnEMF-heavy hospital), this drain leaves the infant’s **cytochromes unprotected** from isotopic poisoning.

**2\. The "Bolus vs. Leak" (The Dielectric Gate)**

Nature’s "slow leak" of MK-4 is a **topological necessity**.

**The MK-4 Shield:** MK-4 is the specific "mammalian hardware" required for carboxylation and the regulation of calcium (a key signaling ion).

**The "Short Circuit":** A massive bolus dose is a "high-voltage" event for a "low-voltage" system. If the baby’s detoxification system (the "software") isn't fully online, the sudden surge of menadione and the subsequent glutathione depletion create a **biophysical blackout**. This "ossification" of early neural pathways is exactly what I have linked to developmental issues like autism in Quantum Engineering #45.

**3\. The Evolutionary "Antithesis"**

The centralized medical assumption that babies are "born deficient" is a misunderstanding of **nature's rate-limiting step**.

**The Placental Filter:** The placenta isn't failing to transport K; it is **actively filtering the frequency** of its delivery. It prefers MK-4 because it’s already "pre-processed" mammalian hardware that doesn't require the toxic menadione bypass.

**The Modern Mismatch:** As I've noted, modern diets are low in K2 (MK-4), but the centralized solution, a high-dose synthetic K1 injection, ignores the **topological insulator** (the placenta's logic). It forces a "Phase 1" metabolic event in a system designed only for "Phase 2" assimilation.

**My Decentralized Summary: The Hardware Solution**
My conclusion is to be wise Mom needs to focus on **maternal MK-4 loading pre-pregnancy and intrapregnancy** (through diet) respects the **Placental/Mammary Gatekeeper**. It allows the mother's more robust biophysical "fab plant" to handle the isotopic fractionation and menadione conversion, delivering only the "clean" MK-4 semiconductor to the baby at a coherent, "low-noise" rate.

**DISCUSSION**

This post pinpoints the **"Systemic Ground-Out"** that defines the modern centralized pediatric experience. By applying first-principles physics, we can see that the medical "standard of care" is effectively a series of high-frequency stressors that dismantle the infant’s dielectric shield during the most critical period of "hardware" assembly.

**1\. The Vitamin K Shot as the "Initial Surge"**
In the hospital environment, saturated with **nnEMF** from monitors, Wi-Fi, and LED lighting, the infant is already struggling to maintain a coherent water table.

**The Multiplier:** The K1 bolus forces the liver into an immediate, high-energy metabolic bypass (conversion to MK-4 via menadione).

**Glutathione Depletion:** This "clears the deck" of glutathione. From a physics perspective, glutathione is the **proton-donor** that maintains the redox potential of the cell. Without it, the cytochromes are left naked against the "illegal mass" of **Deuterium** and the oscillating interference of nnEMF.

**2\. The Tylenol (Acetaminophen) "Short-Circuit"**

The standard advice to use Tylenol for "fever management" after the jab schedule is, biophysically catastrophic.

**Thermal Regulation:** Fever is the body’s attempt to increase the **dielectric constant (_k_)** and speed up enzymatic repair through infrared (heat) emission. Fever's are a hypothalamic de-frag operation and Rockefeller medicine wants it interrupted as soon as possible to sell more products.

**The NAPQI Blow:** Tylenol’s metabolite, NAPQI, is a "glutathione sponge." It doesn't just lower fever; it **extinguishes the pilot light** of the mitochondria. In an infant already drained by the K1 shot and facing 72+ antigenic "software updates," the Tylenol removes the last remaining buffer.

**3\. The Result: "Isotopic Ossification"**

When the glutathione is gone and the nnEMF noise is high, the infant's **Topological Insulator** (the Inner Mitochondrial Membrane) fails.

**The NADD+ Shift:** Instead of NADH, the respiratory chain begins using NADD+ This heavy hydrogen (D) slows down the ATP synthase "motor."

**Brain Failure:** The brain, being the most dielectric-intensive organ, cannot handle this "ossification." The result is a failure of synaptic pruning and myelination, the "blackout" we call neurodevelopmental delay or autism.

**4\. Physics > Pharma: The "Wise" Path**
A "magnetic excursion" requires **higher magnetic coherence**, not more chemical interference.

**The Mother's Role:** Nature intended the mother to be the "Fab Plant." She processes the K1into (MK-4), filters the (D) through her own metabolism, and delivers a **"Quantum-Clean"** fuel through breast milk.

**The Rejection:** Rejecting the bolus-dose paradigm in favor of maternal loading is the only way to respect the **Biochemical Lagrangian** of the infant.

**SUMMARY
Given these boundary conditions, this is why I see the Vitamin K shot as a "nnEMF-multiplier" that further destabilizes the baby's already-stressed dielectric water table in a hospital setting. When you add this shot to the 72 jab schedule it is no wonder little brains fail to develop post natally in a magnetic excursion. We need to be advocating for doing everything right, not advocating half assed ideas.**
We are indeed in a **Topological Race**. Centralized medicine is trying to solve a hardware problem with flawed software patches, while the real solution lies in restoring the dielectric integrity of the "biological computer" from day one.

**SUPPOSED INFECTION**
I’m seeking community input on _Protomyxzoa rheumatica_ illness versus environmental illness. I tested positive in 2011 via Dr. Stephen Fry’s proprietary methods, but I have lingering doubts about its legitimacy given the lack of independent confirmation. _Protomyxzoa rheumatica_ is reportedly a protozoan that is vectored by mosquitos. Presumably, virtually everyone is exposed via mosquito bites, but only those persons missing a particular protein become actively infected. Using Alter.systems AI, I recently found that there is a likelihood that the protozoan may incorporate into itself genetic material from mold. As such, mycotoxin contamination is common in sufferers. Essentially, Fry reports that it excretes biofilm (like Lyme Disease) and colonizes the vascular system within the biofilm.

**TECH ABUSE vs PATHOGEN ILLNESS**
After 35 years of "tech abuse" to date (indoor engineering career, bad light, EMF exposure), I’ve struggled with mild-moderate peripheral neuropathy since 2007 and in 2010 things got worse with chronic fatigue, feeling like shit, and post-exertion malaise (pain and exhaustion).

Uncle Jack got me to ask the question: **Is this pathogen a root cause or is my body simply failing or possibly letting Protomyxzoa live due to environmental/deuterium stress?** Has anyone else navigated this?

I've recently fixed my blue light exposure, electricity, and EMF in my environment, never miss a sunrise, lots of midday sun, earth/soil grounding, live in low density outside of Scottsdale, AZ in an area of 1+ acre lots. I'm improving, but still not fully well.

**GROUNDING**
I live in a desert with depth to groundwater at 320 feet below ground surface, so grounding is likely poor. I could grow a little grass. Otherwise, I want to construct an outdoor grounding area. My plan is locate a spot with minimal surface (I have 5g cellular) and no detectable subsurface electrical or magnetic interference. Then drill three 8 ft deep holes in a triangle pattern about 8 ft apart, insert an 8-ft long copper ground rod in each hole and backfill with bentonite clay, brass clamp insulated copper wire leads to each copper ground rod and connect the 3 wires to a central hub / grounding mat, keep the bentonite moist to at least 8 feet with a drip irrigation emitter. Can anyone comment, modify, or add to the above ideas to improve grounding?

I plan to check out El Salvador late summer or fall of this year.

Thank you!!

**GROUDING**
The one rod to measure sounds like a good idea, but is it to evaluate the selected site prior to construction or is it the whole plan? Here is my detailed site evaluation generated by AI is below. What do you think?

Voltage Mapping (Differential Testing)

Establishing the Reference Ground: To measure the "quietness" of a location, you need a stable Earth reference.

The "Gold" Standard: Drive a small, temporary 2-foot stainless steel stake into the ground at the very edge of your property (the point furthest from the house and all utility lines). This is your Reference Ground.

The Measurement:

Set your DMM to AC Millivolts (mV).
Connect the COM (black) lead to your Reference Ground stake using a long, insulated wire.
Hold the red probe (or attach it to a small metal plate placed on the soil) at various points in your yard.

Interpreting Results: You are looking for the point where the AC voltage difference between your yard probe and the Reference Ground is the lowest.

Why: If you measure a high voltage difference, that location is under the influence of an external electromagnetic field. The lower the Vdiff, the more "electrically neutral" the spot is.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-05-07T23:55:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/female-ejaculation-deuterium-depletion-of-the-ls-plexus.32721/post-373378>

**FEMALE EJACULATION: DEUTERIUM DEPLETION OF THE LUMBO-SACRAL PLEXUS**

A few consults this has come up but no one wants to talk about in the journals so I decided to post a thread about it.

Female ejaculation, or squirting, is a real biological phenomenon, though it's surrounded by myths. It's not entirely urine, but the expulsion of a clear fluid during intense sexual arousal. Sometimes it's completely mistaken for urine. This event is directly related to the deep stimulation of the G-spot (an extremely sensitive area on the anterior vaginal wall) and the degree of activation of the parurethral glands of Skene, the functional counterpart of the male prostate.

**"Squirting" comes from a specific gland:** Female Prostates: Skene's glands are a series of glands and ducts located around the female urethra, just behind the pubic bone. They are rich in a specific type of prostatic antigen (PSA). PSA is a protein also present in male ejaculation.

**Deep Stimulation is a de-frag Rx:** When the G-spot (a highly vascularized area in the urethro-vaginal space) is stimulated with sufficient intensity and pressure, these glands become more active. Urethral Expulsion: Skene's glands secrete a clear fluid that accumulates in small sacs and is expelled through the urethra in a jet-like manner during orgasm due to the contraction of the pelvic floor muscles. Why is it so different from other types of orgasm?

**Clear and Odorless Fluid:** The fluid released from Skene's glands is clear and has a different chemical composition than urine. It contains glucose, urea, creatinine, and PSA, but in very different concentrations than urine. Sometimes, this fluid may be accompanied by varying amounts of urine.

Muscle Contractions dopamine/melanin link: The expulsion of the fluid is accompanied by strong contractions of the pelvic floor muscles, acting as a "pump" to release the accumulated fluid. Not everyone experiences squirting because many women do not have perineal melanin because they put tarps over their gardens because of societal conventions. The intensity of stimulation, individual anatomy, and psychological relaxation are key factors.

**MEDICAL NOTICE** It is important to know that squirting is a normal and healthy sexual response and should not be confused with urinary incontinence. Unveiling this phenomenon could be important so that women can explore their sexuality without shame. I believe this is another format to release deuterium from the lumbo-sacral plexus and helps avoiding hthe risk of CSF lattice lock in females. (ALS/MS)

# Thread: Ex tech slave journey to savage

### bulkbiceps — 2026-05-08T19:16:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ex-tech-slave-journey-to-savage.32724/post-373461>

I used to be like many of those dopamine zombies into all the latest hand held slave collars, "smart" home prisons etc. and unfortunately it took a vaccine injury to jolt me awake to it all. Was in the cave a very long time....
That was 6 yrs ago, I'm 42 now and up to that point I was an automation controls technician working in the oil industry as a sub contractor then once the company I worked for closed their doors I moved to the armpit of Ohio that is Cincinnati to work for Teva Pharmaceuticals as an instrumentation calibrator. Yeah, two industries I now resent......for good reason. Anyway, my personal nightmare began when I was 24yrs old beginning with alcoholism derived afib as I can best guess to be the cause. Two radiofrequency ablations stopped most of that but my issues didn't resolve fully with svt at times. But the worse part of my nightmare didn't start in earnest until I developed Hashimoto's, adrenal insufficiency, POTS, GI problems and the constant fatigue. All likely brought on by moronic compliance. Even had a small bought of myocarditis in 2022. How I've managed to still be around I don't know. The adrenal problem got bad enough my endo placed me on a hydrocortisone pump, at the height I was running about 30mg a day and needing an er visit sometimes multiple times a week for months on end. What really led me to start going down the rabbit hole of decentralized approaches was what my functional endocrinologist told me that the nnemf was likely a driver for many of my problems. That was around 2 yrs ago when she told me that after I had been through a multitude of so called "specialists" before finding her. Sure as shit, ditching all the wireless devices in the house and using my engineering knowledge to clean up the high frequency noise in my home wiring practically eliminated all the adrenal crashes/arrythmia bouts. So since that time I've been back to the hospital maybe a handful of times barely countable on a hand in a few years. Big improvement over multiple times a week.

Granted, I'm sure I am still living on little borrowed time anyway given the context of what led me to this point but still want to improve conditions and myself. I've incorporated as much as I can so far of what I've been reading here but being unemployed with pennies to my name doesn't tend to leave room to do everything. So, buying DDW, getting a Magnetico mat with a grounded sheet connected to the steel beams driven into the floor in the basement is even not an option for me now. Sunlight at least is doable, that's easy. Same with a cold shower in the morning as I have Swedish/Chzeck/German ancestry but I haven't started doing that just yet.

I'm not on any Thyroid meds presently as I've been able to keep my numbers in the normal/optimal range using iodide supplementation so far. I developed a peanut/fish allergy mid 2021 to the point I would break out in a full body rash if I had either one. I've not tried having any of it since then nor seafood since I'm not keen on having to inject myself with steroid immediately following an epi pen for the adrenal crisis that would cause. So I've just avoided the situation altogether.

Still have major issues with POTS, especially if I kneel down and stand back up again and major exorcise intolerance from sitting on my ass for 6 yrs. That and coupled with some major medical PTSD from being through all of it makes me fearful of any kind of exorcise.

And the wife is still an overweight, PCOS pre-diabetic tech junky addicted to the phone that no matter what I say falls on deaf ears.....not sure what I can do there other than part our separate ways.

Moving southward to El Salvador would be nice but I'm broke and don't speak a lick of Spanish so I've consigned myself to nature's eventuality for the region.

### DavidL — 2026-05-09T07:24:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/understanding-vortices.32685/post-373530>

Is there any reason for a person to use MB or creatine if they are running a light water and 160 dielectric constant?
If someone is living these Rx's, would there really be a need for this stuff. Yes I know for sure MB as you've told us in the past can be a miracle when you know how to use it. But creatine, other than building muscles, what's the point?

I did I dive on creatine:
Creatine really took off as a commercial bodybuilding supplement in the **early 1990s**, especially after the 1992 Barcelona Olympics and the 1993 launch of branded creatine monohydrate products like EAS Phosphagen. It became a supplement because research showed it was stored in muscle and could boost short‑burst power output, which translated into more reps, heavier lifts, and faster muscle and strength gains for bodybuilders.

Creatine was first isolated from meat in 1832 by French chemist Michel Eugène Chevreul, who found it in skeletal muscle.

In 1912, researchers at Harvard showed that ingesting creatine increased muscle creatine content, hinting it could work as a dietary enhancer rather than being immediately excreted.

By the 1920s, human studies showed that about 10 g per day for a week increased muscle creatine stores and had anabolic‑type effects (promoting muscle growth).

From the 1950s–1980s, synthetic creatine became cheap, and lab and animal work showed roles in muscle growth and preventing atrophy, but this was mostly within research settings, not mainstream bodybuilding.

When bodybuilders really started using it:

-   There are scattered anecdotes of lifters using “sweated beef” or meat extracts to get more natural creatine, but this was niche and unstandardized.
-   The big shift came in the early 1990s after key human trials showed oral creatine supplementation significantly raised muscle creatine and improved repeated high‑intensity performance.
-   Creatine hit public and bodybuilding consciousness when Olympic champions like Linford Christie and Sally Gunnell publicly credited creatine use around the 1992 Barcelona Games.
-   In 1993, EAS introduced Phosphagen, one of the first mass‑market creatine monohydrate products specifically sold for strength and muscle performance; this marks the start of creatine as a standard supplement in bodybuilding circles.
-   By the 1996 Atlanta Olympics, estimates suggested a large majority of athletes (often cited as “over 80%”) were using creatine, reflecting how fast it spread through strength and power sports.

EAS (the sports nutrition brand that made Phosphagen) was founded by **Anthony Almada** and **Ed Byrd** in 1992 in Pacific Grove, California. A few years later, bodybuilder and entrepreneur **Bill Phillips** bought the company from Almada and Byrd and became the sole owner until it was sold to private equity (North Castle Partners) in 1999, and then later to Abbott Laboratories in 2005.

Bill Phillips is an American fitness entrepreneur, author, and former CEO/owner of the supplement company EAS, best known for his book **Body‑for‑LIFE** and his role in popularizing scientifically marketed sports nutrition in the 1990s.
Full name: William Nathaniel “Bill” Phillips, born September 23, 1964.
Known as the author of the bestseller _Body‑for‑LIFE: 12 Weeks to Mental and Physical Strength_, often cited as one of the most commercially successful fitness books ever.
Also wrote _Eating for Life_, _Transformation_, and related Body‑for‑LIFE materials and journals.

## Role in EAS and the supplement boom​

-   In the early 1990s, Phillips was publisher and editor‑in‑chief of _Muscle Media 2000_ (MM2K), a hardcore bodybuilding/strength magazine with a big influence on supplement culture.
-   EAS (Experimental & Applied Sciences) was founded in 1992 by Anthony Almada and Ed Byrd; Phillips started writing about their creatine product (Phosphagen) in his magazine after seeing its potential.
-   In 1994 he bought EAS from Almada and Byrd and became 100% owner, using his MM2K audience and long‑form, “editorial style” ads to turn EAS into one of the dominant supplement brands of the 90s (Myoplex, Phosphagen, HMB, etc.).
-   He sold his majority stake to North Castle Partners in 1999 for around 160 million dollars, kept a minority share for a few more years, and was eventually fully out before Abbott Labs acquired EAS in 2005.

You end up triggering the very **singlet-state trap** you are trying to avoid.

This is why I gave the warning two years ago when I traveling the globe looking at the slope of the line for the magnetic decline. Taking MB in a magnetic decline zone is a Rx for death.

You risk severe **mitochondrial overheating** and accelerated "Maillard-browning" of your neural tissues.

> It merely forces the stalled gears to grind harder against the sludge. You are running your 9,000 RPM motor on a negative T-2 buffer with a chemical accelerator. Eventually, the circuit breaker (MITF-AMPAR) will trip permanently. This is the exact same risk as creatine use.
> By expanding my thesis to include exogenous phosphocreatine donors, I've exposed the fundamental blind spot of the entire "nootropic" biohacking movement.Allopathic sports science and neurology treat creatine as a "pure fuel" that simply donates a phosphate to recycle ATP. However, in a low-dielectric, high-deuterium environment, it functions as another **high-risk chemical accelerator** that forces a stalled engine to grind against itself.
>
> **_Creatine_** operates in the brain by crossing the blood-brain barrier via the [SLC6A8 transporter](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5231321/). Once inside, it increases the pool of phosphocreatine to rapidly buffer ATP demands during stress.

> **The False Focus:** Just like methylene blue, this provides a noticeable "burst" of mental energy. It artificially raises the [ATP/ADP ratio](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4585235/), creating the illusion that cognitive function has been restored.

> **The "Sovereign" Ruling on Methylene Blue/creatine**
>

> 1.  You must already be on **92.5 ppm DDW** \[1000000/155 = 6451\] to ensure the "speed bumps" are far enough apart.
> 2.  You must be **physically grounded** to a normal-polarity magnetic anchor to act as the "sink" for the forced electron flow.

### AlbertW — 2026-05-10T00:36:31+0300

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=373199)
>
> The core issue is neither the declination angle itself nor its movement, but rather what a "magnetic stall" (a significant drop in the Earth's **dipole moment**) represents for the planet's atmospheric chemistry.
>
> Your intuition about the poles being "static" in Siberia vs. "moving" hits on a key geodynamic reality: the **alignment** of magnetic and physical north (\\(declination = 0\\)) is a marker of a strong, stable geodynamo. When they decouple significantly, it often signals a weakening of the entire protective field.
>
> **Why Declination Matters (Biophysically)**
> The alignment of the magnetic dipole with the rotational axis (True North) is not just a convenience for human evolution; it is a byproduct of a **strong, active core**.
>
> **The Protective Shield:** A strong magnetic dipole acts like a "filter" that prevents solar wind from stripping away the atmosphere.
>
> **The Oxygen Link:** As shown in the _Science Advances_ article I provided you for the 100th time now, there is a powerful correlation between **magnetic field strength** and **atmospheric oxygen levels** over the last 540 million years.
>
> **The "Magnetic Stall":** If the magnetic north pole were to "permanently end up in Siberia" while remaining static and strong, it might not be a problem for humans. However, such a "static offset" is physically unlikely. In geophysics, when the pole drifts far from the rotational axis (high declination) or wanders erratically, it usually indicates the **dipole is weakening** or "stalling".

I want to make sure I get this right.

**Does magnetic declination angle really matter?**

Based on my reading, whether a place has 0 or +5 or -5 magnetic declination doesn't really matter. As you mentioned above, if the magnetic north pole were to permanently settle in Siberia, then many places could have a permanent +10 magnetic declination and that would be perfectly fine and animals would adapt. Imagine these two places:
\- Location A: close to equator, +5 magnetic declination, but because it is farther from the magnetic north pole, the time derivative of declination angle is small
\- Location B: close to pole, 0 magnetic declination, but because it is closer to the magnetic north pole, the time derivative of declination angle is massive
Couldn't Location A be potentially better than B?

**What really matters**
It just seems like the important thing is this: core magnetic field is weakening, and we are able to learn that from SWARM satellite data and the fact that the magnetic north pole is moving rapidly. As it's weakening, some places suffer because they literally lose a lot of overall magnetic field intensity. Other places suffer because the first (or second, or third) time derivative of magnetic field vector is changing so much that animals are having trouble adapting. Because magnetic field is changing, we want to "connect" to the deep Earth as much as possible. One way to do that is to live near active volcanoes. They provide excellent grounding to improve SNR ratio, and the entire ecosystem around it remains healthy (Jack talks about volcanoes and their magnetic flux "pinning" clouds in tropical regions), so there are second-order benefits, like humans being able to eat food that is un-deuterated.

I'm still confused about "horizontal shearing" of field lines though. See [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/...erations-for-es-other-areas.32661/post-373191](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pole-shifts-ciss-considerations-for-es-other-areas.32661/post-373191)

Isn't "horizontal shearing" even worse near the poles, since time derivative of horizontal isogonic line movement is much worse there than at the equator, no? I don't understand how volcanoes help directly mitigate horizontal "shearing." It seems like horizontal "shearing" (or movement) is unavoidable. We are just hoping that active volcanic flux will provide so much benefit to humans that we can tolerate the horizontal "shearing." Am I misunderstanding? Are active volcanoes actually somehow "preventing" horizontal shearing?

Are active volcanoes actually somehow "preventing" horizontal shearing?

The critical distinction between a **static offset** (declination) and a **dynamic flux** (the time derivative). Animals and mitochondria don’t care about the _map_ (declination); they care about the _rate of change_ and the _coherence_ of the field.

If the magnetic field vector is shifting rapidly, it creates "magnetic noise." This lowers the **Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR)** for the OPN5 and Ca2+ resonance.

The "horizontal shearing" you are confused about is essentially a **torsional strain** on the magnetic field lines.
Imagine the magnetic field lines as guitar strings. In a stable environment, they are straight. As the core weakens and the poles shift rapidly, these lines "bend" or "shear" horizontally across the Earth's surface.
**The Biological Cost is an impedance mismatch.** This shearing creates an **impedance mismatch** between the Earth's magnetic field and the "water table" of your cells. If the field lines are shearing, the atoms in your lattice **harmonic** or the **K+ resonance** becomes "blurry." This is the "magnetic bankruptcy" where the mitochondria can no longer "clamp down" on oxygen because the field they are trying to couple with is physically twisting.
**Volcanoes** provide a solution to this shearing because they magnetic pin you down. In physics, flux pinning occurs when a superconductor (or a highly conductive geological feature) "traps" magnetic field lines, preventing them from moving.

Active volcanoes, with their deep magma conduits and high mineral content, act as **"Magnetic Anchors."**They "pin" the local magnetic flux, shielding the surrounding ecosystem from the "horizontal shearing" happening elsewhere.
By pinning the flux and stabilizing the local field, these areas maintain high **oxygen tensions** and "structured water." This allows the ecosystem to naturally filter **Deuterium** (the heavy hydrogen that "breaks" the mitochondrial motor), leading to the un-deuterated food supply. The entire country of El Salvador is a Volcano that is quite young. That also offers advantages because it has no memory of the last magnetic flip 780,000 years ago.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-05-10T02:55:39+0300

The Permian survivors have told us a story and I think the Neanderthals missed it, but two groups of Homospaiens have not. This wisdom is telling me the Permian might have also been a serious magnetic excursion event.

Magnetic Hysteresis is the **"Isotopic Memory"** of a failing system, and it is the definitive signature of the Permian "Great Dying." If you look at the geological record through the lens of a "Magnetic Stall," the Permian wasn't just a CO2 event, it was a **Planetary Waveguide Collapse.

1\. The Hysteresis Loop: The "Memory" of the Stall**

In physics, **Hysteresis** is the lag between a change in a system (the magnetic field) and the physical response of the material (the iron/water lattice).

**The Permian Loop**: Data from the P-Tr boundary shows a massive "Magnetic Reversal Frequency" spike. The planet was "flickering" like a dying lightbulb.

**The Lag**: The "Heavy" isotopes (D & T) didn't just appear; they accumulated in the "lag" of the hysteresis loop. Because the field couldn't "re-sync" fast enough, the **150 ppm silt** "welded" the global water lattice. This is why there was a 96% kill rate.

**2\. The "Hard" vs. "Soft" Survivors**

Hysteresis tells us who really survives a "Magnetic Reset":

**Magnetic "Soft" Materials**: These lose their alignment (coherence) the moment the external field drops. This was the 96% of life that vanished—the "T-Rex" models of the Permian. They did not know how to tap atomic hysteresis yet.

**Magnetic "Hard" Materials**: These have high **Coercivity**. They "remember" their internal spin even when the external "Ground" vanishes. This is the **Archean-Bacterial Fusion** we discussed. The survivors had enough **Endogenous Melanin/DHA Torque** to maintain their own "Internal Sun" during the planetary asystole. The Egyptians and Mayans found this answer in my opinion.

### Slow_cheetah — 2026-05-09T13:25:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-go-wrong-between-deuterium-being-loose-and-gone.32727/post-373535>

I believe I have the Redox Rx environmental foundation down: Living in a strong magnetic field. 92.5 ppm DDW, ketogenic diet, grounding every day.

**What i think i know:**
DDW and ketosis reduce incoming deuterium.
And mastic gum mechanically shakes lattice-locked deuterium loose piezoelectric.
When it’s loose the body needs to get rid of it. The exhausts are: sweat, melanin (as a sink), and the glymphatic-cervical lymphatic axis during sleep.

Been tracking my sleep for 2 weeks and my REM is well below minimum advised (30-50min in total in a sleep of 7t:30m)

**What i don’t know:**
If the glymphatic pipe is the primary brain deuterium exhaust and it runs during REM (and my REM is essentially flat) does that mean my brain's deuterium exhaust is blocked?
If one has Redox Rx down, do you need interventions to open the exhaust pipes before the de-fragging or does fixing the Redox Rx foundation open them?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-guru-problems-many-blame-glucose-is-it-wise.23142/post-373565>

> [AntonisK said:](/goto/post?id=373097)
>
> Just a quick question that came to mind recently after reading uncle Jack's recent posts on deuterium...
>
> As "we know" - actually what we found out is a better statement - after many decades of empirical data on millions of athletes/trainees, consuming liquid glucose polymers (maltodextrin, dextrose etc.) along with whey protein right after a weight training session helps replenish muscle glycogen stores and speed recovery. In other words, this strategy works...
>
> But, since maltodextrin is loaded with deuterium, I wonder if this approach is beneficial at all. After all, the last thing we want is load the muscle cells with deuterium. If so, what do you think would be the best alternative way to achieve this (glycogen replenishment & help recovery)?

Sorry but are we talking strict Resistance Training?

Unless you are doing multiple sessions per day and only concentric exercises, you will literally never depelte your muscle glycogen storages. This is a local process.

Taking malto or other stuff you mentioned is a very low iq mammal thing to do. There is multiple research for RT and glycogen depletion. No one should take creatine or the stuff you mentioned to replenish the creatine phosphagen or glycogen tanks. These 2 do it alone and in an absolutely fast way. EVEN without any carbs.

Think - 1) food usually is not purely isolated unless processed (eg. Whole food will still have a carb).

2) cory cycle
3) gluconeogenesis via excess aa
4) triglyceride breakdown (glycerol backbone)

Sorry this is a biochemistry rhino writeup but its enough to understand why taking any of that for RT, and even for those folks that train multiple times per day, is non sense.

As per protein, yeah, there is plenty of evidence that the anabolic window for elite athletes is shortened (sandwich 2 meals on workout and within 6 hours). MPS will be high for 24-48 for beginners and intermediate meatheads.

And yes, meatheads are never advanced, hence they inject peptides and take steroids because they are deuterated.

Aside all the deuterium thing and the health implications of this, many decades of empirical data have shown without any doubt that post exercise liquid carbs (along with whey and/or EAAs), help restore muscle glycogen stores faster and speed-up recovery from intense weight training, especially high volume hyperthorphy sessions, leading to increased muscle mass.

It has been tested and cross-checked numerous times by many strength trainers around the globe, there is no doubt it works big time for the majority of people that fall in the above category.

Sure, there are other approaches too, but this has proved to be the quickest way, like it or not.

Anyway, I'm not saying it's safe (quite the opposite), that's why I wanted to know - by asking here - if there's a better (in terms of safety) way to achieve this, that's all!

Its not safe and the speed element is not needed for most of the population. Not even for elite natural weightlifters if they optimize the real recovery variables - WHICH IS NOT GLYCOGEN REPLENISHMENT FOR RT when OXPHOS and creatine phosphagen do a lot of the heavy work while lifting. Again, we talk strict RT, even combo sets with some super set mix.

WlSo is carbohydrate intake completely irrelevant for strength trainees? No, there are 2 scenarios in which carbohydrate intake may be important and you and I agree:

1) When performing over 10 sets per muscle group.

2) When training twice on the same day with a high training volume.

How many people do this? 1) is for meatheads which have no clue of how body works and structuring a plan and 2) is for elite athletes or enhanced in the vast majority of cases.

Light, water, magnetism and a well designed training plan (volume per week and frequency) + whole food diet covers you FULLY (specifically the complete protein and threshold of 1.6-1.8g / kg as per multiple studies).

It is another story if you are doing another sport (think tennis). I stress this so you or your friends dont fall for the scam of RT supplements.

I do this for a living at high level, I know a bit more than those influencers shilling their discount codes.

Cheers

Not even for elite natural weightlifters if they optimize the real recovery variables - WHICH IS NOT GLYCOGEN REPLENISHMENT FOR RT when OXPHOS and creatine phosphagen do a lot of the heavy work while lifting. # Thread: *help needed to navigate this space* New Gold member - adrenal fatigue, thyroiditis/hypothyroid, chronic fatigue (ex vegan, now carnivore)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-needed-to-navigate-this-space-new-gold-member-adrenal-fatigue-thyroiditis-hypothyroid-chronic-fatigue-ex-vegan-now-carnivore.32730/unread>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Beccy — 2026-05-09T23:34:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-needed-to-navigate-this-space-new-gold-member-adrenal-fatigue-thyroiditis-hypothyroid-chronic-fatigue-ex-vegan-now-carnivore.32730/post-373580>

Hi, I am almost 50, was vegan for 12 years, had a lifetime of allergies, IBS, chronic fatigue, pernicious anaemia, and a passion and drive for a profession which is extremely physically and mentally challenging (travelling equine podiatrist consultant and coach). I have tried dozens of diet and healing protocols and never felt I have achieved anything close to my potential to be well, and therefore have never felt I had the resources to fulfill my life’s purpose. The will to do so keeps me seeking answers and solutions and fighting the good fight for optimum health and wellness for optimum longevity.

I am currently 135lb, eat mostly lamb, salt and spring water, and am 5 foot 4 inches. I am self employed and have slowed down my schedule this year due to my stressful career since December in order to prioritise healing. I dont drink alcohol or smoke.

Since radically changing my diet, most of previous chronic and periodically acute symptoms have gone, such as IBS.

My biggest challenges currently are energy, consistent sleep, or a lack of both, brain fog and memory issues. I wish to be creative and productive, fit and active once more, and yes, wear my clothes comfortably without the extra pounds gained in the past few years. I wish to contribute more to supporting my family….

What got me here…

I had my last gallbladder attack 10th Dec 2025, and then started IF (16:8) and slowly switched to paleo with meat over the next 6 weeks. I experienced the keto high in January, lost 10lbs and felt great. Then crashed. On 1st March, I went pure carnivore, stopped drinking caffeine and taking progesterone HRT. I had 4 weeks of sleep hell and zombie energy, and from 1st April, received support from a carnivore naturopath who recommended 4 part saliva cortisol and more thyroid tests (I had them done in February and realised I was hypo and inflamed - “on the away to Hashimotos“. I discovered I am low cortisol - but following correct daily curve, just low. The naturopath recommended natural desiccated thyroid and adrenal cortex supplements and these Increased energy and function literally overnight. This was In April. My thyroid T3 level is normal anccording to repeat blood test early May and I haven’t re-tested saliva yet.

I found this site after exploring why I am gaining weight again (not muscle - fat/water) and have worrying lymphedemia - this is eased by rebounding and daily lymphatic massage on 7 key points. I lost 8lbs in 24 hours by doing this. However I am not getting any additional info from the carnivore community I am in and am very dedicated to regaining my health and life, and achieve my life goals. I also know I feel better in the sun, hence my attraction to the work Dr Kruse does and melanin.

I live in northern England. I try and sunbathe (naked) whenever I can, and I practice good sleep hygiene. I recently tracking health stuff with a Hume pod and wearable device for better objectivity in my health.

I am keen to learn and somewhat overwhelmed by all this new and complicated information.

\* How do I use the membership best to begin to navigate my way to health? I am overwhelmed \*

That’s me!

### wtsmg — 2026-05-11T16:03:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-the-webinars-on-different-rx-new-gold-member.32733/post-373734>

The Webinars aka Q&As aka PowWows are found on a different site/platform, [https://optimalklubs.com/](https://optimalklubs.com/), where you'll have to log in; you'll also find the Blogs there. Jack used to put out webinars with a Q&A and now it's Q&As without a webinar. It's hosted differently from the Forums (where we are, here, [https://forum.jackkruse.com/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/)). There are a couple of other related web address (kruselongevitycenter.cm, kruseatdestin.com) that get redirected around, usually to the Forums or Optimal Klubs where, if a member, you log in. The specific webinars for various Rx are scattered in the older years of the webinars (you'll have to search and with Gold, you should have access to them).

There was a shift recently to a new server, I think, so some links and such may still be in the process of being sorted out. If something doesn't look correct, try refreshing your page or clearing the cookies and cache on your device and log in fresh as it may be trying to load the old stuff.

### Szen — 2026-05-11T16:31:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-the-webinars-on-different-rx-new-gold-member.32733/post-373736>

I still maintain a google excel listing most of the blog/webinar links, in case its needed.

# Thread: Single Mitos Looking to Meet Others in this Group
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/unread>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tiffanyfinnegan — 2026-05-10T14:59:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/post-373623>

Hi all, looking to start a way for some single people to meet each other in the event there’s a possible love connection. As I’m sure many of you experience, especially once you dive deep into Dr Jack’s material, it can be hard to find a partner who understands and wants to share a similar lifestyle together. Just gonna start this off to see if there is interest and then we can figure it out from there!

This isn’t geographically specific yet - we’ll figure that out once we start a group of sorts as I’m sure many of us may be moving at some point anyway.

### FDDio — 2026-05-10T16:06:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/post-373631>

Nice Survey, I’ve been thinking about how best to arrange a meetup in the Greater Toronto area, not just for singles, but for all Mitos, so it will be good to see how your meetup idea develops. For those of us who are in relationships, it’s still difficult to find like-minded people to have meaningful conversations with and enjoy good times.

And FWIW, having been single well into my 40s, I found the meetups I joined that weren’t just targeting singles, but also included people in relationships, were much better to attend, especially if they revolved around an activity. Why? There was less pressure and awkwardness around just meeting “that one special person.” In a mixed group, people were generally more relaxed/less phony, and connections/relationships grew more organically. (Mind you, I could be showing my bias again, as I met my wife at a beach volleyball meetup after giving up on deceiving dating apps and annoying speed dating events.)

### tiffanyfinnegan — 2026-05-10T16:32:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/post-373637>

Yeah, I’m sure eventually, if there’s enough people for meetups, then absolutely wouldn’t just be single people. I think here we have to create the community virtually first because so many of us are scattered so many places and ultimately if there’s any successful pairings, then hopefully their lives allow them to live near each other. I for one work remotely so I have a lot of flexibility to move and I’m sure within a few years many may consider moving based on the magnetic fields!

### tiffanyfinnegan — 2026-05-10T16:34:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/post-373638>

>
> And FWIW, having been single well into my 40s, I found the meetups I joined that weren’t just targeting singles, but also included people in relationships, were much better to attend, especially if they revolved around an activity. Why? There was less pressure and awkwardness around just meeting “that one special person.” In a mixed group, people were generally more relaxed/less phony, and connections/relationships grew more organically. (Mind you, I could be showing my bias again, as I met my wife at a beach volleyball meetup after giving up on deceiving dating apps and annoying speed dating events.)

Also, I just added a poll option for people like you  and maybe get some friends to join us if you have any like us that are single

### Jack Kruse — 2026-05-10T17:22:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/post-373644>

Friends may have friends that need the connection, FYI

Sounds like a poll for swingers.

### Runnerfireman — 2026-05-10T17:33:50+0300

I’ve been following dr jack for around 8 years and As I learn more and live the life it becomes increasingly difficult to find someone to connect with. I went to a El Salvador last winter for 3 months and met some in the Mitos at Atami beach And have kept in touch with a few. My trip was a roller coaster, I somehow got a mild bronchitis, I had my cell phone stolen, and then I had a horrible reaction with hives that I had no idea where it came from. But, I am determined to go back to El Salvador next winter and hopefully have a better experience. Your idea to somehow connect single likeminded mitos is genius!!! Definitely keep me in the loop as to how this might develop ☺️

### DavidL — 2026-05-10T20:26:11+0300

There is no better relationship hack than leaving the lost in the dust!

# Thread: taVNS for seizures
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tavns-for-seizures.32739/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kachi — 2026-05-11T19:27:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tavns-for-seizures.32739/post-373755>

Taking a cue from the blogs I am planning to buy a taVNS for my 22 year old son with seizures.

My question is about frequencies. I've found a company in Germany (I am in Croatia) that is willing to customize its device to a set frequency in addition to the preprogrammed frequencies. The device already has four programs ranging from 1-100hz with various pulse widths and all have stimulation intervals (eg. 4 sec on/4off) so none are constant. The studies on taVNS for seizures are limited but most used between 20-50hz with various pulse widths, intervals and session duration.

Any thoughts on a preferred frequency? Pulse width? Would it be better to do sessions with constant stimulation for x minutes? Intervals of x seconds on x seconds off?

Any thoughts are greatly appreciated.

(I may be overthinking this).

Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2026-05-11T23:31:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tavns-for-seizures.32739/post-373772>

I'd ask to make it fully tunable so you could figure it out empirically when you use the device at home which works best. I'd also consider a homes TMS unit if the neurologist has targeted the area of the neocortex that is involved you could use the TMS ring over this area. Have the neurologist put a non erasable magic marker on his head so you know where it is when you get home and then you can even tattoo that area with a dot to mark it permanently to so you can target it daily.

### jaedyn — 2026-05-12T02:44:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tavns-for-seizures.32739/post-373792>

> [Kachi said:](/goto/post?id=373755)
>
> Taking a cue from the blogs I am planning to buy a taVNS for my 22 year old son with seizures.
>
> My question is about frequencies. I've found a company in Germany (I am in Croatia) that is willing to customize its device to a set frequency in addition to the preprogrammed frequencies. The device already has four programs ranging from 1-100hz with various pulse widths and all have stimulation intervals (eg. 4 sec on/4off) so none are constant. The studies on taVNS for seizures are limited but most used between 20-50hz with various pulse widths, intervals and session duration.
>
> Any thoughts on a preferred frequency? Pulse width? Would it be better to do sessions with constant stimulation for x minutes? Intervals of x seconds on x seconds off?
>
> Any thoughts are greatly appreciated.
>
> (I may be overthinking this).
>
> Thanks

Hi Kachi,

I recently bought this kit - [https://tragusclip.com/product/kit-1/](https://tragusclip.com/product/kit-1/) and have been experimenting on low settings.

I found this website really helpful for setting it up yourself, Andrea is an OT in Australia and is very knowledgeable on this stuff - [https://guidetolongcovid.com/tvns-for-long-covid/](https://guidetolongcovid.com/tvns-for-long-covid/)

### Nathov1 — 2026-05-11T21:44:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/natalie%E2%80%99s-journal-gold-member-h1a1-northeast-tn-no-covid-jab.32741/post-373762>

Lost 5 lbs in 6 days after adding some of the protocols - most of it was fat.

I could only find a place to do .9 % saline and did 1000 mL last week.

I plan to do another 1000 mL this week and continue to look for a place that can do 3 % NaCl solution.

My daylight computer shows up this week.

Using d minder to calculate vitamin D- 58 K IU in last 7 days. Wearing the tiny bikini helps, still sorting out best way to do that. I got very red in a few places last week. I’m getting morning and late afternoon sun for the red light. I could do more I think.

I’ve gotten contact information from the office to help Chris and I plan a trip to El Salvador. We were thinking sometime in the fall.

Listened again to 2016 and 2017 Vermont talk. Read the latest blogs. I plan to reread the latest blogs when I get the daylight computer while I’m outside. I find myself getting distracted while reading. I get what he is saying about dopamine issues and ability to process information.
